user_query:
I need a lean data pack prepared for our performance/security audit. Please read ONLY the files under input/ and produce all artifacts under output/ as follows:

1) JSON minification (no content changes):
- Read input/users.json and input/products.json
- Produce minified single-line JSON files with no spaces after colons/commas and no trailing newlines:
  - output/optimized/users.min.json
  - output/optimized/products.min.json

2) Unified lightweight search index across all input docs (md, txt) and the two JSON files (index their keys and string values):
- Build a JSON object mapping each INPUT filename (basename only) to an array of lowercased keyword tokens (deduplicated). Include entries at least for: users.json, products.json, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and notes.txt.
- Save to: output/index/search-index.json
- Run two sample searches against this index for the queries "optimization" and "security" and save:
  - output/search/optimization.json with fields: {"query":"optimization","results":["<top matching basenames>"]}
  - output/search/security.json with fields: {"query":"security","results":["<top matching basenames>"]}
(Results should list at least two basenames present in the index if found.)

3) Compacted digest:
- Create a concise, deduplicated digest that merges the most important points from input/README.md, input/CHANGELOG.md, and input/notes.txt. Keep it under ~300 lines.
- Include a title line "Compacted Summary" and a "Sources:" section listing the basenames of the files used.
- Save to: output/compact/compacted.md

4) Optimization report:
- Generate a machine-readable summary with exact byte sizes before and after minification for users.json and products.json, the percent reduction per file, and a total_reduction_percent across both.
- Use this structure:
{
  "files": [
    {"original_path":"input/users.json","optimized_path":"output/optimized/users.min.json","original_size_bytes":<int>,"optimized_size_bytes":<int>,"reduction_percent":<float>},
    {"original_path":"input/products.json","optimized_path":"output/optimized/products.min.json","original_size_bytes":<int>,"optimized_size_bytes":<int>,"reduction_percent":<float>}
  ],
  "total_reduction_percent": <float>,
  "timestamp": "<ISO8601>",
  "method": "json-minify+smart-index+auto-compaction",
  "notes": "<short string>"
}
- Save to: output/report/optimization_report.json

Important constraints:
- Do not modify any files in input/.
- Only process the files I listed in input/.
- Ensure all output paths exist and files are valid for their formats.
- The minified JSON must be valid, single-line, and whitespace-minimized (no ": " or ", ").

Files to read from input/: users.json, products.json, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, notes.txt.
